title: Consistency of P1 and P2: locate the construction in the Thm 2.2 / Thm 2.3 gap

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Sub-lemma (G5).** Explicitly reconcile the positive clause (P1) and the negative clause (P2), which superficially look contradictory.

P1: *every* $\pi$ is $\Sigma^{in}_1$-definable from *some* finite tuple. P2: *no* fixed tuple defines all $\pi$, and for each tuple $\bar a$ a defeating $\pi$ exists *even after augmenting with $\pi(\bar a)$*. 

The proof must show there is no hidden contradiction by demonstrating that the finite tuple which defines a given $\pi$ in P1 consists of data (a base point *inside each moved gadget*) that is genuinely **not** recoverable from an arbitrary externally-chosen $\bar a\cup\pi(\bar a)$. This is precisely the gap the authors highlight between Theorem 2.2 (uniform parameters that *determine* automorphisms) and Theorem 2.3 (per-$\pi$ parameters that *define* them effectively). 

Deliverable: a short but rigorous argument that the construction inhabits exactly this gap, so that (P1) and (P2) jointly hold without contradiction.
